compiler_basics.title: What is a Compiler?
compiler_basics.content: A compiler converts high-level programming language code into machine code. It processes the program through multiple phases such as lexical analysis, syntax analysis, intermediate representation, optimization, and code generation.

lexical_analysis.title: Lexical Analysis
lexical_analysis.content: Lexical analysis is the first phase of a compiler where the input string is broken into tokens. Example: 'starts with digit' becomes ['starts','with','digit'].

syntax_analysis.title: Syntax Analysis
syntax_analysis.content: Syntax analysis checks whether the sequence of tokens follows the rules of a grammar.

ast.title: Abstract Syntax Tree
ast.content: AST is a simplified tree representation of the structure of input that removes unnecessary grammar symbols.

regular_expressions.title: Regular Expressions
regular_expressions.content: Regular expressions are patterns used to match character combinations in strings.

dfa_nfa.title: DFA vs NFA
dfa_nfa.content: DFA has exactly one transition for each symbol. NFA can have multiple possible transitions.
